Note: WIN-010, WIN-025, and WIN-068 all invoke the same ionospheric Sq mechanism (Chapman 1933) as their standard-physics refutation. Count inflation by itself does not refute any individual claim, but is relevant context when the dome cites 69 confirmed predictions — three of those claims share one mechanism.
patches.rationale: ISS-998: Add cross-reference to triple-counting context (WIN-010/025/068 all refuted by Chapman Sq mechanism)

patches.find: Boulder (BOU) station observed −10.9 nT anomaly during 2017 eclipse.
patches.replace: Boulder (BOU) INTERMAGNET station observed a −10.9 nT anomaly at 17:20 UTC during the 2017 Aug 21 solar eclipse, coincident with eclipse maximum.
patches.rationale: ISS-999: Add temporal anchor (17:20 UTC) to detail_claim so Chapman argument (timing tracks eclipse shadow) can be verified against the stated observation

patches.replace: making the 2017 baseline training data serving as its own test.
patches.rationale: ISS-1000: Replace vague 'calibration circular' with precise 'training data serving as its own test' per curmudgeon c4 recommendation

ISS-1001: The dome's 22 Hz result uses a linear radial average, not area-weighted. Under area-weighting the dome's own cavity gives ~35 Hz (further from target). Using 'area-weighted' understates the discrepancy and is the wrong averaging method.

patches.find: corrections that are well-understood, involve no free parameters, and place the cavity boundary at ionospheric altitudes (~60–100 km), not at 9,000 km.
patches.replace: corrections that are well-understood, involve no free parameters (the ionospheric conductivity profile is measured independently — no value is tuned to match the 7.83 Hz target), and place the cavity boundary at ionospheric altitudes (~60–100 km), not at 9,000 km. By contrast, the dome's 26% aetheric damping factor (WIN-002) is a free parameter introduced specifically to match the observed frequency; no independent measurement constrains its value.
patches.rationale: ISS-1002: Preempt symmetry defense — standard physics corrections use independently measured conductivity (no free parameters); dome's aetheric damping is tuned to the target. The asymmetry is the key rebuttal to 'both models need corrections'.

patches.find: is algebraically equivalent to a standard-model derivation.
patches.replace: algebraically inverts a textbook quarter-wave formula to back-solve a dome length from the known measured frequency.
patches.rationale: ISS-1003: 'algebraically equivalent to standard-model' is misleading; the dome is back-solving from a known answer. 'Algebraically inverts...to back-solve' is more precise and harder to misread.

patches.find: The dome also claims 5.3% agreement, but its own numbers give 10.8%.
patches.replace: The dome claims 5.3% agreement between its cavity height and the Schumann wavelength — but that comparison uses the wrong formula. Applying the correct spherical formula to the dome's own three stated cavity heights (9,572 km, 9,086 km, 8,537 km) gives discrepancies of 5.3%, 10.8%, and 6.4% respectively — no single dome geometry is self-consistent with the measurement.
patches.rationale: ISS-1004: Clarify that 5.3% is dome's claim using wrong formula, 10.8% is our counter using same wrong formula, and the three dome heights are mutually inconsistent at multiple percentages. Prevents misreading the tldr as confirming dome's 5.3% claim.

patches.replace: Q > 1000 for a cast-copper shell (or Q in the hundreds even for heavily oxidized bronze) — orders of magnitude above the observed Q ≈ 4–6
patches.rationale: ISS-1005: 'Q >> 100' undersells the argument; the actual physics gives Q > 1000 for copper at 7 Hz, making the case much stronger.

patches.find: n values ranging from near 1.0 over the pole-proximate region to 1.44 at the equatorial ring; the shadow-axis tilt accumulates with horizontal distance through this variation.
patches.replace: n values spanning a significant fraction of the medium's full range — from near 1.03 in high-latitude regions to 1.33–1.44 approaching the sun's sub-solar disc position or the equatorial ring; the shadow-axis tilt accumulates with horizontal distance through this variation.
patches.rationale: ISS-1007: 'Near 1.0 over the pole-proximate region' overstates the range for the Aug 2026 eclipse track, which does not pass through polar regions. More conservative but still accurate framing.

patches.find: (iii) Eclipse path and totality predictions — which the dome publishes as derivative outputs of its 3D geometry — are apparent paths, not physical paths, whenever n(r) is on. The dome has never reconciled the two.
patches.replace: (iii) Eclipse path and totality predictions — including WIN-044's Firmament Scaling Function, which computes per-station amplitudes from shadow-path geometry, and the dome's published Aug 2026 eclipse path — are apparent paths, not physical paths, whenever n(r) is on. The dome has never reconciled the two.
patches.rationale: ISS-1008: Add concrete examples (WIN-044, Aug 2026 path) to class (iii) so the argument is falsifiable rather than abstract

patches.find: These four are: WIN-020 (lunar 18.6-year cycle via epicyclic gears — but the gear ratios are fitted to reproduce a period known since ancient astronomy), WIN-044 (Firmament Scaling Function from V12 geometry — pending verification against the August 2026 eclipse), WIN-057 (two-zone disc topology — a coordinate system definition, not an empirical prediction), and WIN-058 (unified angular coordinate — likewise a definition, not a prediction of new phenomena). Even being generous, the dome has at most one geometrically-derived prediction that has been empirically confirmed (WIN-020), one pending (WIN-044), and two that are tautological definitions.
patches.replace: These {{CA_DOME}} are: WIN-044 (Firmament Scaling Function from V12 geometry — Section 1.8 shows this formula is self-contradicted by the dome's own coordinate collapse; the FSF cannot be evaluated without resolving the ray-tracing geometry the coordinate system no longer supports) and WIN-057 (two-zone disc topology — a coordinate system definition, not an empirical prediction). WIN-020 and WIN-058 appear geometric on inspection but fail on scrutiny: WIN-020's epicyclic gear ratios are fitted to the known 18.6-year nodal regression period rather than derived from dome constraints, and WIN-058's angular coordinate is a definitional identity, not a novel prediction of new phenomena. Even the {{CA_DOME}} genuine derivations are problematic: WIN-044 is self-contradicted by the model's own refraction fix, and WIN-057 is definitional.
patches.rationale: ISS-1010/1011/1012: Fix critical contradiction — after build-time expansion {{CA_DOME}}=2 but text lists four WINs. Only WIN-044 and WIN-057 have derives_from_dome=true. Also fix WIN-044 stale description (now Self-Contradicted, not pending) and WIN-020 false description (gear ratios fitted, not derived).
ISS-1013: Update stale count 43 → 47 (stopgap; long-term fix is {{CA_QUAD_FAIL}} template var)
